definitions: A grouping of events, data points, or phenomena that occur close together in time.
In statistics or data analysis, a concentration of occurrences within a specific time frame.
A collection of related items or activities that are observed or analyzed as a unit due to their proximity in time.
examples: The analyst identified a temporal cluster of network intrusions.
Researchers observed a temporal cluster of disease outbreaks.
A temporal cluster of customer complaints indicated a recent product issue.
